sections.paragraph:
-

(1) part. act. seeing, sc. visions, i.e. a seer, a prophet (Seher), compare the root No. 1, letter d, a word anciently used, according to 1Sa 9:9. Κατʼ ἐξοχὴν, it is applied to Samuel, 1Sa 9:9, seqq.; 1Ch 9:22 26:28 29:29 other prophets, 2Ch 16:7, 10 2Ch 16:10.

(2) abstr. i.q. רֳאִי vision (in which sense the accent ought perhaps to be placed on the penultima in the manner of Segolates. Isa 28:7, שָׁגוּ בָּרֹאֶה “they totter (even) in their visions.” Comp. חֹזֶה No. 2.

[(3) with art. הָרֹאֶה Haroeh, pr.n. m. 1Ch 2:52 see רְאָיָה .]
